title: M5 MacBook Pro vs M4 MacBook Air: The Ultimate 2025 MacBook Showdown – Which One Should You Buy?
transcript: In the ever evolving world of Apple laptops, 2025 brings us two major contenders that redefine performance and portability. The MacBook Pro M5 and the MacBook Air M4. These two devices might look similar on the surface, but their goals are entirely different. The ProM is built for raw performance and professional-grade workflows, while the ARM4 focuses on efficiency, portability, and affordability. But which one should you actually buy? Whether you're a video editor pushing pixels, a developer compiling mass of projects, or a student juggling notes in Netflix, today's in-depth comparison will help you make the perfect choice. We'll break down everything: performance, display, design, battery life, pricing, and more in one detailed look at the ultimate MacBook battle of 2025. So, plug in your headphones, relax, and let's find out which Apple machine truly rules the future of laptops. script, single paragraph, SEO optimized, 1,500 words. Choosing between the M5 MacBook Pro and the M4 MacBook Air in 2025 is one of the toughest tech decisions for Apple fans, as both laptops are crafted perfection, but serve entirely different audiences, and understanding which one fits your lifestyle can save you money while boosting your productivity. Starting with performance, the M5 MacBook Pro is Apple's latest powerhouse, featuring a 10 core CPU and 10 core GPU, delivering up to 12% faster CPU performance and 36% faster GPU output compared to the M4 chip in the air. This machine is made for professionals. Think 4K video editors, 3D modelers, AI researchers, and developers who demand speed, stability, and multitasking without compromise. Its memory bandwidth of 153 GB per second ensures lightning fast data transfers while it enhanced neural engine supercharges AI based tasks like photo recognition and real-time rendering. In contrast, the M4 MacBook Air isn't built to compete in raw performance, but rather to deliver Apple's signature balance between efficiency and power. With its fanless design and optimized thermals, it runs whisper quiet and handles daily workloads like document editing, streaming, video calls, and light photo editing with effortless smoothness. The Air M4 is Apple's go-to laptop for students, travelers, and anyone seeking portability without the bulk of the Pro. When it comes to displays, the M5 MacBook Pro boasts a breathtaking 14.2 2 in liquid retina XDR display with promotion technology offering a 120 Hz adaptive refresh rate and a peak brightness of up to 1,600 nits. Perfect for color critical work, HDR video, and immersive entertainment. The deep blacks, wide color gamut, and buttery smooth motion make a creator's dream screen. Meanwhile, the M4 MacBook Air comes with a 13.6 6 in LED back lit retina display maxing out at 500 nits and a 60 Hz refresh rate. More than adequate for most tasks like web browsing, typing, watching movies, or editing photos, but lacking the visual depth and fluidity of the Pros XDR panel. Now, let's talk design and portability. The M4 Air wins here hands down. Weighing just over 1.2 kg and maintaining Apple's sleek, wedge-free chassis, it's perfect for mobility. Slip it into a backpack and you'll barely notice it's there. Whether you're working from a coffee shop, university campus, or an airplane seat, the AirM4 fits anywhere your lifestyle takes you. On the other hand, the M5 Pro is slightly heavier and thicker, but for good reason. It houses an advanced thermal system with dual fans to handle sustained heavy workloads without throttling. It's crafted from durable aluminum with a solid, professional feel that creatives and developers will appreciate. The Pro's sturdiness ensures longevity, making it an ideal choice for those who need a reliable workhorse rather than a portable companion. Connectivity is another area where the Pro M5 dominates. Featuring three Thunderbolt 4 ports, an HDMI port, and an SD card slot. Perfect for professionals dealing with external monitors, cameras, and media storage, the M4 Air keeps things minimalist with just two Thunderbolt 4 ports, catering to users who rely primarily on wireless accessories or minimal setups. Both laptops feature Wi-Fi 6E and Bluetooth 5.3, ensuring strong connectivity and futureproof performance. When it comes to storage, both support blazing fast SSDs, but the M5 Pro offers higher read/write speeds, a blessing for large video projects or design files that demand instant loading and saving. The Air M4, though slightly slower, is still fast enough for daily workflows and app launches, offering the signature snappiness that Apple silicon is known for. Moving to battery life, both devices shine, but with slight differences. The M5 MacBook Pro delivers up to 24 hours of video playback and 16 hours of web use, while the M4 Air offers an impressive 18 hours of video playback and 15 hours of web browsing. Both ensure all day performance, but the Pros larger battery and optimized efficiency give it a slight edge, especially for professionals on long shoots or trips. In the audio and webcam department, both devices impress with the M5 Pro featuring a six- speakeraker setup with spatial audio and a studio quality 1080p camera, while the AirM4 offers a four speaker system that's clear and loud enough for meetings, plus the same 1080p FaceTime HD camera. Now, when it comes to price, this is where the decision becomes clear for many buyers. The M5 MacBook Pro starts at $1,700, making it a premium investment meant for those who truly need its capabilities. The M4 MacBook Air, on the other hand, starts at $999 with student and seasonal discounts occasionally dropping it closer to $800, making it one of the best value laptops Apple has ever made. So, who should buy which? If you're a professional content creator, developer, video editor, or 3D designer, the M5 Pro is your best bet. Its raw power, stunning display, and versatile port selection make it a productivity beast that will last you for years. But if you're a student, writer, or casual user looking for a reliable, lightweight machine that delivers premium Apple performance without breaking the bank, the M4 Air is a smarter pick. Both devices share Apple's flawless ecosystem. instant handoff, seamless syncing with iPhone, and the incredible Mac OS and experience. But the ProM simply caters to a more demanding crowd. Ultimately, your choice depends on what you value most. Portability and affordability or professional-grade performance and longevity. Our final verdict. If you want the best balance between power, portability, and price, go for the MacBook Air M4 as it offers unbeatable value for most users in 2025. But if you need uncompromising power, stunning visuals, and future proof performance, the MacBook Pro M5 is worth every penny. Either way, Apple continues to dominate the laptop space with these masterpieces, offering something for everyone. So, which one will you choose? The Fedite efficiency of the M4 Air or the raw power of the M5 Pro? Let us know in the comments. And if you found this comparison helpful, don't forget to subscribe because we bring you the latest Apple insights, leaks, and comparisons every week, only on Techverse, where technology meets clarity and passion. If you're torn between performance and practicality, here's the rule of thumb. Go with the MacBook Air M4 for value and portability, or the MacBook Pro M5 for raw power and futureproof performance. No matter what you choose, Apple's M series keeps pushing boundaries and we'll keep you updated with every innovation only on Techverse.

title: 💻 M5 vs M4 MacBook Pro - Don’t Waste Your Money! #m5macbookpro #m4macbookpro #macbookpro #m5macbook
transcript: The M5 MacBook Pro is here, but should you save your money and get the M4 instead? Design practically identical. Same 14.2 in chassis, Magic Keyboard, and notch. The M5 is slightly heavier thanks to updated internals, but you won't really feel it. Ports same as before. Three Thunderbolt 4, HDMI, SD cards, Mags Safe. Performance, the M5 is faster. CPU up 15%, GPU up 45%. Storage speeds doubled. But for everyday activities like browsing, photo editing or video, the M4 already handles it effortlessly. The Pro just shines for heavy workloads and sustained performance. Display, audio, battery, basically the same. Same gorgeous miniLEDD XDR panel, six speaker system, and all day battery. Price: M5 starts at $1,599. M4 at $1,269 refurbished. Nearly identical experience for hundreds less. Verdict: For most people, the M4 is a smarter buy. Only go M5 if you need top tier GPU power or extra storage.

title: Apple M5 Era Begins: New iPad Pro, 14-inch MacBook Pro & Vision Pro 2025 Unveiled
transcript: Apple has just unveiled a massive lineup of new devices, and this might be one of the biggest updates we've seen in years. The company officially released the next generation iPad Pro, a redesigned 14-in MacBook Pro, and an upgraded Apple Vision Pro, all powered by the new M5 chip. With this update, Apple is signaling a new era of higherformance computing that leans heavily into artificial intelligence and immersive spatial experiences. All three products are now available starting today through Apple stores, the website, and the Apple Store app. Let's start with the iPad Pro. Apple calls it the most advanced iPad ever made, and it's easy to see why. The device comes in 11in and 13in sizes, both featuring a stunning ultra retina XDR display with tandem OLED technology. The new M5 chip boosts AI performance by up to three and a half times compared to last year's model and more than five times faster than the original M1 iPad Pro. That translates into lightning fast performance for creative and productivity workflows from 3D design to professional video editing. Connectivity also gets an upgrade thanks to the new C1X modem and N1 networking chip for faster wireless speeds and lower latency. Running iPad OS 26, the new iPad Pro takes advantage of intelligent AI features like smart scene selection, improved multitasking, and faster app responses, making it a true mobile powerhouse. Next is the 14-in MacBook Pro, which brings Apple's professional laptop line to a whole new level. With the M5 chip inside, performance jumps dramatically, up to six times faster than the M1 MacBook Pro and more than three times faster than last year's version. The GPU architecture delivers around 60% better graphics performance, while storage speeds have doubled, meaning faster exports and smoother playback for heavy creative projects. The design remains sleek and familiar. But the Liquid Retina XDR display now offers an optional nanoexture glass finish to reduce glare. You still get the 12 megapixel center stage camera, six- speakeraker spatial audio, and a wide range of ports including HDMI, SDXC, and Mag Safe. Battery life is another standout feature. Apple claims up to 24 hours of use, the longest ever for a MacBook Pro. The device runs Mac OS Tahoe, optimized for AI processing, performance efficiency, and seamless syncing across the Apple ecosystem. Then there's the Apple Vision Pro, which now joins the M5 family. This update brings major gains in speed, clarity, and comfort. With the M5 chip, rendering is smoother, graphics are sharper, and AI powered interactions are noticeably more responsive. Apple also introduced a new dual knit band for better comfort during long sessions, responding directly to feedback from early users. Running on Vision OS 26, the updated Vision Pro supports over a million iOS and iPad OS apps, thousands of games, and an expanding library of 3D movies available through Apple Immersive. It's clear that Apple is positioning the Vision Pro as more than a niche product. It's becoming a central part of the company's long-term vision for spatial computing. All three products are available right now through Apple's online and physical stores. Customers can trade in eligible devices, configure custom MacBook Pro options, and purchase accessories or Apple Care Plus coverage. Vision Pro, however, isn't part of the trade-in program. Apple is also offering 3% daily cash rewards with Apple Card purchases and 0% APR, monthly payment plans for those who want to spread out the cost. To make things easier, the company is continuing its today at Apple sessions to help customers set up their new devices and learn how to use the latest features. In short, Apple's 2025 lineup, the iPad Pro, MacBook Pro, and Vision Pro represents a unified leap forward powered by the M5 chip. It's not just about faster processors. It's about building an ecosystem where every device is AI ready, seamlessly connected, and designed for the next generation of computing. Whether you're a creative professional, a student, or a tech enthusiast, these updates show that Apple is betting big on a future driven by performance, intelligence, and immersion. So, what do you think? Is Apple's M5 era the start of something revolutionary, or just another incremental upgrade? Share your thoughts in the comments and don't forget to like, subscribe, and hit that bell for more deep dives.

Apple's Mac Studio 2026 isn't for everyone, but for professionals, it might be the dream setup. The 2026 Mac Studio marks a key moment in Apple's silicon journey. Just a few years ago, critics wondered whether Apple could sustain innovation beyond the M1 era. The company's focus is shifting from raw performance to intelligent performance. where AI acceleration, energy efficiency, and war flow optimization all merge into one unified experience. So, if you've been holding out for that one upgrade that truly feels next generation, 2026 might be the year to make your move. The Mac Studio 2026 isn't just faster, it's smarter, quieter, and built for the AI age. And as Apple continues to merge power with intelligence, one thing's for sure, the future of computing doesn't belong to the biggest machines anymore. It belongs to the smartest.

Not just a device capable of pro work, but a device designed for it from the silicon all the way to the software. The M4 iPad Pro with its tandem OLED screen was a test. It was Apple asking, "Can we make a screen better than our MacBooks?" The answer was yes. The M5 iPad Pro is the follow-up question. Now, what if we put the power of a MacBook Pro and the brain of a supercomput behind that screen? But what about the rest of the device? Apple isn't just going to put a new chip in the old body. The M4 iPad Pro was impossibly thin. So thin, in fact, that some people were worried. The M5 model isn't about thinness. It's about utility. We're expecting the first major redesign of the form factor itself. There are whispers of a new material, not just aluminum, maybe a carbon fiber composite or a new titanium alloy that's both lighter and more rigid, solving any Bengate fears. And the ports. Oh, the ports. The single Thunderbolt port is a bottleneck. It's the bottleneck for professionals. The M5 iPad Pro could be the first to feature two Thunderbolt 4 ports, one on the side and one on the bottom. Think about that. You can have it docked to your external display and hard drive, while also having a second port free for a camera, a microphone, or an SD card reader without needing a clumsy, stupid dongle. And the display, the Tandem OLED in the M4 is stunning. It's the best screen on any device, period. So, how do you beat it? You don't. You evolve it. We're talking about a matte anti-reflective option straight from the factory. A true nanoexture glass just like on the Pro Display XDR. A screen that you can actually use in bright sunlight or under harsh studio lights with zero glare and a new Apple Pencil. The Apple Pencil Pro was a great step with the squeeze gesture. But the M5's Pencil, we're hearing about haptics, not just a simple vibration. We're talking about dynamic haptics. Imagine drawing on your screen. When you select a charcoal brush, the pencil drags with a subtle, gritty friction. When you select a marker, it glides. When you're sculpting in 3D, you can feel the clay pushing back against your digital tool. This is no longer just a stylus. It's a direct tactile interface with your digital work. It bridges the gap between the physical and the digital. Let's talk about the Magic Keyboard. The current one is good, but it's heavy and it's cramped. The new Magic Keyboard for the M5 Pro will be the final piece of the laptop killer puzzle. It'll feature a larger glass trackpad with haptic feedback, identical to a MacBooks. It will have a full row of function keys and crucially it will be engineered with a new hinge mechanism that allows for more viewing angles including a studio mode where the screen lies almost flat for drawing without having to take it off the keyboard. So you have an M5 chip with a tuninometer process and a revolutionary neural engine. You have a new Pro OS that merges iPad OS and Mac OS. You have a new body with dual Thunderbolt ports. You have a new nanoexture screen. And do you have a new haptic pencil and a full-size keyboard? What does this all mean? It means that for the first time, the post PC device that Steve Jobs dreamed of is finally here. For a photographer, it means you can go on a week-long shoot with just your iPad. title: M6 MacBook Pro LEAK — Apple’s Biggest Redesign Yet!
transcript: Apple is preparing a major leap forward with its next generation of MacBook Pro models. Currently known by their internal code names K114 and K16. These laptops are expected to be thinner, lighter, and powered by the allnew M6 chips, a lineup that will skip the M5 redesign phase entirely. According to recent reports, Apple is targeting a release window as early as late 2026, roughly a year from now. To understand the shift, it's worth noting where things currently stand. The base 14-inch M5 MacBook Pro is already on the market and has impressed users with incredible performance benchmark scores show it reaching over 4,300 in single core and around 18,000 in multi-core performance. Meanwhile, the high-end M5 Pro and M5 Max models, internally known as J714 and J716, are due in early 2026. But what's surprising is how soon the redesigned M6 generation will follow just about 9 months later, signaling Apple's rapid pace of innovation. One of the most exciting upgrades for the M6 MacBook Pro is the switch from miniLEDD to tandem OLED displays, similar to the one used in the M5 iPad Pro, which many reviewers have called the best display ever. This change will bring incredible brightness levels of up to 1,600 nits, perfect black tones, zero blooming, and an even faster response time, making the 120 Hz's refresh rate feels smoother than ever, especially in gaming and animation. For the first time, Apple is also introducing a touchscreen display in a MacBook Pro, marking a historic shift in its laptop design philosophy. Alongside that, the familiar notch at the top of the screen will disappear, replaced by a small hole punch camera cutout. There are several reasons why many believe it might be worth waiting for the M6 MacBook Pro instead of buying an M5 model. The first is performance. Built on TSMC's revolutionary 2nome gaffet gate all-around fed technology, the M6 chip will be far more efficient and powerful than previous generations. This new architecture minimizes power leakage and allows Apple to pack in more cores, pushing performance to new heights. The new display and dynamic island also give the laptop a distinct identity. Removing the notch and introducing an interactive software feature will make the M6 Pro instantly recognizable as the latest version, which naturally boosts its desiraability. This ties into another reason to wait, resale value. When Apple last introduced a major redesign in 2021, older models saw their used prices drop sharply. Experts expect the same to happen again when the M6 arrives as buyers rush to own the first MacBook Pro with OLED, a touchscreen, and dynamic island. Battery life is also expected to see massive improvements thanks to the efficiency of 2 nanome GAF chips and upgraded components such as N2 generation Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and a C2X modem for 5G connectivity. Apple can deliver unprecedented endurance. And as always with Apple, there's room for surprises. The company often saves a few unannounced features for the final reveal, such as new ports, exclusive hardware capabilities, or unexpected camera upgrades like the 18 megapixel center stage sensor that caught users offguard before. So that's it for this video. Feel free to subscribe to us for more tech content. Thanks for watching.

title: MacBook Pro vs MacBook Air 💻 ¿Cuál elegir? Las DIFERENCIAS me han sorprendido...
transcript: After the release of the MacBook Pro with the M5 chip, you're probably thinking about buying one and you're not sure whether to get the MacBook Air or the MacBook Pro. Both have very similar chips, but there are differences beyond that.  I'm going to tell you which one I would choose and why. [Music] Obviously, everything will depend on each person's situation, what you're going to use it for, but let's look at the differences, and in terms of money, in terms of price, you should know that one is the MacBook Air with 256 GB of SSD storage and the MacBook Pro D52. I think we should increase the MacBook's storage capacity to at least 512 GB, not only to match both devices, but also because 256 GB is going to last us very little time, especially if we don't have an iCloud plan or an external hard drive. So once we put both devices at the same storage level, the price becomes a little more equal.  There is still a difference, but I think this is relevant because both also start with 16 GB of RAM.  So now, let's look at the differences.  We're going to analyze the base 13-inch and 14- inch devices.  In this case we have differences, the most important externally being in the ports, since the Mabook Air only includes the MaxSave port for charging and two Thunderball USBC ports located on the left side.  While the MacBook Pro also includes this Max port for charging, it includes three Thunderball USBC ports, two on the left and one on the right, as well as an HDMI port and an SD card slot.  This is especially important, for example, in my case, because I no longer just use the USBC ports, but rather the SD card slot, since I use it every day, for example, to record.  If I had to use an adapter it would be a bit of a hassle.  Even so, if we adapt, this shouldn't be a major problem, unless you need it in your daily life. The MacBook Pro is heavier, by about 300g, taller, wider, and overall, the MacBook Air is considerably lighter, especially for those of you who will be moving around a lot with it.  Even so, both devices can be easily transported and this won't pose a major problem either.  But something very important that I take into account is the screen, and it's not just the screen size that we can modify and choose different versions, but rather the screen technology, especially the brightness.  The MacBook Air starts at a brightness of 500 nits compared to the 1000 nits of the MacBook Pro with SDR content.  And what's more, the MacBook Pro does take this into account, and we will be able to see HDR content and even have more brightness under these conditions.  Therefore, if you are going to work in bright conditions, where the sun shines directly on the screen , you should keep this in mind, since obviously 500 nits is sufficient when we have good indoor conditions, shade and so on, but when the light hits it directly , having much more brightness will give us better sharpness and better screen quality, because the technology is also different.  We're talking about the MacBook Pro having a mini LED screen, and therefore the quality is also much higher when viewing content.  We also have a promotional display, so you'll see smoother content as long as it's adapted to this larger technology on the MacBook Pro than on the MacBook Air.  As I said, there are quite a few differences on screen, and this is important for you; you should also keep that in mind.  But before we get to the differences in the chip itself, let's talk about cooling.  The MacBook Air doesn't have internal ventilation, while the MacBook Pro does.  What does this give us as a result?  Well, in intensive tasks, especially long-term ones , we'll see that the MacBook Pro runs cooler and also sustains performance better than the MacBook Air for extended periods or during very intensive tasks, both on the GPU and CPU. Therefore, you won't experience that thermal throttling, which is basically the MacBook Air lowering its performance over time to prevent the temperature from rising too much.  As for the camera, it's the same on both devices. 12-megapixel camera with overhead view, with center stage and will follow us around the room whenever we have it available.  But we're going with the chip because today I'm going to compare the MacBook Pro with the M5 chip against the MacBook Air with the M4 chip.  Is there that much of a difference?  Well, be careful because I've been comparing them under different conditions and these are the results. To begin with, if we look at CPU performance, we have a difference of approximately 15% compared to the M4. Geekbench tells me that yes, indeed, that 14% is present in the CPU, both single core and multicore.  And where we find the biggest difference is in GPU performance, which is 45% faster, especially in the long term, as shown in this 3D Mark Life Stream test, which effectively measures that 45% speed.  And if we even use games with ray tracing, you should know that the difference increases to 63%. We also find tests of a widely used application for rendering and modeling, such as Blender. The benchmark shows a 26% improvement in this MacBook Pro with the M5 chip.  If you want to know if SSD storage is also faster, it is indeed.  And if we compare both devices with 1 TB of storage, we find that if we want to transfer more files and faster, we will be able to do it on the MacBook Pro in a faster way. But above all, where does this MacBook Pro excel?  This is when we use AI-powered applications that run directly and locally on our device.  And here we have, for example, the Geitbench AI test, which if we run it with the CPU, we will have a difference of 14 or 13%, but if we use the GPU with those neural accelerators that the MacBook Pro has with the M5 chip, we go to a difference of 47 to 130%. The same applies when we create images directly with models on our device.  And here with the Draw Things application we see the differences in seconds between these two devices or even with the downloaded Google YMA model using the OLAMA application, that is , an AI model downloaded on our device, we see the speed of the M5 compared to the M4.  So if you're going to use applications like Final Cat, applications that have significant GPU performance, graphics performance applications, or even development applications, you won't find that much of a difference in CPU performance, but you will find a difference in GPU performance, especially when you apply a lot of work for a long time.  However, both are compatible with fast charging, but we will have to buy the charger separately.  For example, in the European Union, the MacBook Pro does not come with a charger at all, and we can buy that 70W or higher charger in the store itself.  The MacBook Air does come with a compatible charger that doesn't provide fast charging, although we can choose that 70W charger that does provide fast charging on the MacBook Air.  So now I'm going to give you my final conclusion.  If you want a device for productivity, for working on intensive tasks from time to time, but not on a daily basis, or even for any semi-professional work, the MacBook Air with the M4 chip will be perfectly sufficient. The configuration depends a little on you, but at least 512 GB of storage and 16 GB of RAM.   This will give you a device that will last for several years.  However, if you want a device for semi-professional or professional use every day with intensive tasks in different areas, for example, work and productivity applications, the MacBook Pro will give you that extra performance that you will only get in the Pro versions thanks to that ventilation, and to this is added the improvements in screen and the convenience of the new ports.  So, as I said, it depends on each case, but that would be my conclusion.  Now it's your turn. Tell me in the comments which one you chose and why, if you're happy with your decision.  And as always, rate the video if it helped you.  Follow me on the WhatsApp channel, follow me on social media, that's where I share all the latest news about these devices and much more.  Also, subscribe to the channel so you don't miss anything, it helps me a lot.  And we'll see you in the next one.  Bye.

title: iPad Air M4 OLED DISPLAY! - New Surprise For Everyone
transcript: Apple's iPad lineup has never been this confusing or this exciting. With the new iPad Pro already running on the cuttingedge M5 chip, users are now asking one key question. What happens to the iPad Air? After all, it's now two generations behind, stuck with the M3 while its premium sibling races ahead. But according to fresh leaks and insider reports, the next iPad Air is shaping up to be more than just a modest refresh. Even if some long-awaited features are still off the table, the iPad Air only got its M3 upgrade in 2025 with no real design overhaul. The same flatedged aluminum body, Touch ID power button, and dual size options, 11in and 13in, remain unchanged. The only notable difference was support for the new Magic Keyboard, though that's an optional extra. Despite that, the Air continues to be Apple's most balanced tablet. Thin, light, and powerful enough for most users with an excellent value proposition, especially the 11in model, which still undercuts every other Mchip device in Apple's lineup. Now, the big question is what Apple plans next. According to multiple, the iPad Air M4 won't be getting OLED just yet. That technology will stay exclusive to the iPad Pro for a few more years. Instead, Apple seems to be working on a more advanced LED panel, not quite OLED, but better than the current liquid retina LCD. The first air models with OLED are reportedly not expected until the M6 or M7 generation, meaning users looking for those deep blacks and ultra thin displays will have to wait until around 2028. Under the hood, the M4 chip will be the star of this update. Built on a 3nanometer architecture, it's more efficient and powerful, promising not just a performance bump, but longer battery life, too. The M4 brought impressive gains to the MacBook Pro, and with the iPad Air's design staying the same, Apple could use that efficiency to squeeze out even more screen on time. However, it's unlikely that the Air will feature the full M4 configuration seen in the Pro. Apple will probably bin down the chip slightly just as it did with the M3 Air, meaning a 9 core CPU and a 10 core GPU are the most likely specs. As for RAM, the M3 iPad Air shipped with 8 GB, but considering the iPad Pro M5 and iPhone 17 Pro lineup both now start with 12 GB, it would make sense for Apple to follow suit. It's not confirmed yet, but leaks suggest Apple might finally boost the base configuration to 12 GB for better multitasking and future iPad OS features. That would be a significant improvement, especially for creative users who rely on multiple heavy apps. Apple's road map for 2026 indicates the new iPad Air is scheduled for release in the first half of 2026, likely around March or April. Aligning with the brand's usual spring refresh cycle, a summer reveal at WW WDC remains possible if internal testing faces delays, but it's unlikely to slip beyond that. Pricing thankfully should remain consistent. Thanks to stable component costs and unchanged tariffs, sources close to Apple's supply chain claim no major adjustments are expected. The 11-in iPad Air M4 is expected to start at $599, while the 13-in variant should begin at $799. Both for Wi-Fi only models.

The multi-core score is up to 18.01 points, which is about 20% higher than last year's MacBook Pro Ship M4. The previous model scored 14,726 points, but this is where it gets interesting. The M5 ship tested this time is an M5 chip that uses a 10-core CPU, divided into 4 high-performance cores or PCOS and 6 energy-saving cores or ECOS, which may still have a more powerful model. Just imagine how high the scores would be if the model used more PCES. The PA Pro Pro using SIP M5 was also tested and scored.   They are close to 4,175 points for slee and 15,577 points for multicoree, which shows that the M5 chip is designed to work well on both MacBook Pro and Pro models. For anyone who is waiting for the arrival of the new MacBook Pro and Pro models that come with the M5 chip, you have to follow the news closely because Apple has not yet announced the date. Officially sold and this is the part Many people may overlook how Apple was able to develop the M5 to be so powerful.   It could have a huge impact on the PC market in the future if Apple continues to move forward.   As they continue to develop their own chips, we may see a MacBook Pro that can beat high-end PCs. title: iPad Pro M5 开箱：去年评测有个结论要改一下
transcript: Alright, everyone In front of me now is the newly released iPad Pro with the brand-new M5 chip version Let’s unbox it together Here is a USB-C charging adapter and a black USB-C to USB-C cable Ta-da The black version of the iPad Pro with a nano-textured screen The key upgrade this time is the M5 chip Although it’s just a chip upgrade it involves changes in four areas Let me quickly walk you through The first area is the improvement in GPU graphics processing power The increased processing speed allows smoother operation of gaming and graphic software and enhances AI task performance (3.5x improvement over M4) Ray tracing capabilities in games and VFX software have also improved (1.5x improvement over M4) The second upgrade is in memory For the 256GB and 512GB versions the memory has been upgraded from 8GB to the current 12GB allowing more tasks to run simultaneously while staying smooth Memory bandwidth increased by 30% (153GB/s) This allows for smoother operation of AI large language models locally without requiring internet The third area of improvement is in communication capabilities For the first time on the iPad Pro M5 Apple uses its self-developed C1X modem which claims faster transmission speeds (50% faster than C1) and lower power consumption (30% less than the previous generation) Now, let’s quickly show you how fast the new Apple self-developed C1X modem can download and what its current download speed on this iPad is Currently the eSIM on this iPad is slightly different from a phone’s As far as I know, they use different licenses The iPad eSIM has a tablet license while the iPhone eSIM has a phone license There are several core differences The first difference is if you activate the iPad eSIM feature in theory you don’t need to visit a physical service center But if you want to activate the phone eSIM feature you must visit a physical service center in person with your ID The second difference is that for phone eSIMs you can directly continue using your previous mobile plan and phone number But for iPad eSIMs you need to activate a new number The third difference you can pay independently through a phone app the iPad eSIM number must be tied to the phone number and you need to pay for the iPad through the phone First download the China Unicom app on your tablet Go to Services and choose to replace your SIM card Select Tablet Products Choose Apple iPad Then click the input box here It will automatically fetch your device information After entering your eSIM card number choose to replace the card data Then go through live authentication It will start downloading the eSIM card to your iPad Now the iPad has been activated with China Unicom's 5G network Next, let’s test how the download performance of the C1X modem performs Let’s begin Starting It got faster 1,000mb now Do you still remember earlier this year when we tested the iPhone 16e which was Apple’s first device using a self-developed modem At that time, it only reached 500-600mb But now we can see the download speed has reached a level that’s basically on par with Qualcomm modems Now both the iPad Pro and iPhone Air are using Apple’s latest C1X modem and can achieve full-speed downloads The fourth improvement brought by the M5 chip is charging speed This time, it supports a 40W charging adapter with 60W fast charging (Most of the time charging at 40W) The above four scenarios are the changes brought by the M5 chip this time Since no other aspects have changed today’s video I want to share again two brand-new experiences during the past year of using the iPad Pro These are things I haven’t shared with you in previous videos The first experience relates to a conclusion I made during last year’s review At that time when testing the nano-textured screen I concluded that although the nano-textured screen has excellent anti-reflective performance in terms of absolute contrast it lags behind the standard glass version and is more suited for users who need color accuracy in complex lighting environments But over the past year I’ve realized that environmental light reflections are everywhere On planes, there’s glare On high-speed trains, there’s glare Whenever I take the iPad outside most scenarios are filled with light pollution So for a while I switched to using the nano-textured glass iPad for a period of time The experience was incredibly good In today’s video I want to revise my conclusion from a year and a half ago If you frequently use your iPad on the go then the nano-textured glass offers significant benefits in daily use As for the drawbacks of nano-textured glass they’re actually the same as I mentioned in last year’s video In well-lit environments when displaying absolute black it’s not as pure as regular glass (contrast is slightly weaker) The second drawback of nano-textured glass is that it’s more delicate in daily use So Apple specifically includes a microfiber cloth in the packaging for cleaning purposes Now I’m just hoping that Apple will release an iPad mini Pro someday upgrading the iPad mini to an OLED screen and adding a nano-textured screen option That would be even more helpful for me The second new experience is also somewhat related to the nano-textured screen The iPad Pro has become, over the past year, the best device for reading books and magazines The experience is truly amazing I must share it with you The app I opened is ComicGlass I’ve introduced it before Over the past year I first scanned many classic Disney artbooks Because I personally really love those classic Disney animations After watching "The Lion King: Mufasa" movie my passion for "The Lion King" was reignited Take a look This is the scanned version of "The Lion King" At the time, I also really liked Disney’s art style Each page is incredibly beautiful "Tarzan" I found that this book isn’t particularly thick but it’s packed with information It’s a bit like a storyboard for the movie Reading this artbook feels a bit like being at Hong Kong Disneyland where Tarzan has a treehouse And for example, one of my favorites, "Aladdin" The song "A Whole New World" in this book is honestly one of my favorite Disney songs ever This is how these books originally looked You can see they weren’t cheap back then Each was priced at 30 yuan These books all have hard covers and it’s really hard to carry even one book around especially when it’s a thick stack of books Now, these books are all stored in a thin iPad like this See I even feel that after scanning them the result actually looks even better Take a look at this color comparison Since I’ve already disassembled these books so they can only be placed here in bits and pieces The reading experience is fantastic It feels just like paper with no glare at all And for example, when I switch to landscape mode in landscape mode it becomes like reading two pages at once So how much does it cost to buy such a book secondhand? Let me tell you This "Lion King" book was originally priced at 30 yuan I bought it for 5 yuan Isn’t that a good deal? Now let me show you another one I also scanned this DK’s Children’s History Encyclopedia Kids might find this book a bit too advanced but for me, it’s just right This is the "DK Children’s History Encyclopedia" This book isn’t just hard to carry around even reading it at home requires finding a proper place to put it Otherwise, it’s too heavy to hold But now, take a look This encyclopedia is now fully stored in an iPad Pro that’s only 5.1mm thick What I love most about these encyclopedias is that they contain incredibly beautiful hand-drawn illustrations These exquisite hand-drawn images are irreplaceable by photos or photography Do you know how much I paid for this book? The original price was 138 yuan But I bought it on a secondhand platform for just 29 yuan I scanned this "Children’s History Encyclopedia" which has over 400 pages along with the "Disney Classics" series a total of 10 books Altogether the scanning cost was 67 yuan Such a thick stack of books and someone disassembles the books for you and scans them for you Then they send you a ready-to-use packaged document All you need to do is drag the file directly into your iPad At this point, some of you might say disassembling the books like that is such a waste But the scanning shop also offers book re-binding services (3 yuan per book) This one was originally crowdfunded on Modian I also had it scanned into my iPad Pro The shop disassembled this book for me You can see the traces of disassembly Then they re-bound it for me In addition to the "Children’s History Encyclopedia" I also scanned my personal favorite "Prehistoric Life Encyclopedia" which is also by DK Let me show you Such a thick book Honestly every time I read it, it feels like a spiritual cleansing It’s like I need to burn incense, bathe, and change clothes before I’m ready to read it I’ve stored all these books in my iPad Pro and have truly taken them all over the world Now let me share with you two more books that I personally think are fantastic when scanned into the iPad One is "Secrets of the Battleship" and the other is "Secrets of the Castle" These are also by DK They use large-format illustrations to show you cross-sections of castles This is a cross-section of a castle And when I zoom in on the image you can see the details of the illustration I feel that illustrations are truly a very, very important element in books For example, the castle toilet is positioned here and the waste is discharged downwards Some assassins used to climb up through this drainage pipe to target and assassinate their victim One assassin was climbing up the drainage pipe but got stuck midway and ended up dying in the pipe What a tragic way to die Oh my goodness This has to be one of the worst ways to go I can zoom in as much as I want I feel that this feature is even better than reading the original book In the original book, I had to lean in to see the details This book, "Secrets of the Battleship," talks about how sailors lived aboard battleships back in the day It shows how they stored rum below deck and kept coal and other supplies in certain areas I think using an iPad to read books especially artbooks like these with exquisite layouts and illustrations is an absolutely fantastic use case These two points are some of the new feelings and experiences I’ve had over the past year while using my iPad Pro I wanted to share them with you I hope this can provide some inspiration and reference for you Finally, let me briefly talk about purchase advice The first question 11-inch or 13-inch? To put it simply if your budget allows and you mainly use it in portrait mode go for the 11-inch If you use it in landscape mode more often, choose the 13-inch The second question Should you choose Space Black or Silver? I personally recommend Silver because its bezels look visually narrower Additionally the silver back is less prone to fingerprints And it pairs well with your silver iPhone 17 Pro to create a matching silver set The third question How to choose storage capacity? For everyday use, 256GB is sufficient If you need to store images then consider 512GB If you need to store video content you can go for 1TB The fourth question Should you choose the nano-textured screen? My advice is to visit an Apple Store and check out the actual product first The fifth question Should you go for the cellular model? My personal suggestion is if you need it for work such as handling WeChat work groups or Lark messages (requiring quick typing with a keyboard) posting articles with images or for other time-sensitive work-related tasks that involve a keyboard Then you can consider the cellular model If you mainly use educational apps or video apps then there’s no need to choose the cellular model Lastly, regarding protective cases I want to emphasize again be cautious when buying full-cover cases especially those that feel very tight when fitting and are even harder to remove I’ve seen too many users accidentally bend their iPads while trying to remove such cases Of course it might not always be the user’s fault Sometimes you might wake up to find your iPad bent by itself overnight Alright, that’s all for today’s video A simple unboxing and sharing my personal experience I hope this provides some references and helps you fully explore the potential use cases of the iPad I’m Zhong Wenze See you in the next video

title: Mac mini M4はモバイルに使えるか！？MacBook Airと比較 外出先 出張 旅行 ノマド リモート モバイルモニター
transcript: Hello.  This is Ogawa Da.  I purchased the Mac Mini M4 in November 2024 and have been using it for almost a year.  The Mac Mini M4 is so small that I thought it might actually be possible to use it as a mobile computer in various places around the house or even for work outside, so I would like to test it out.  I usually use it as a desktop computer next to a large monitor at home, but when I want to use my Mac at work, I consider combining it with a mobile monitor and using it as a mobile computer . When using the Mac Mini M4 on the go, the key point is a mobile monitor .  I use a Keeptime 2K 13.5in mobile monitor.  I previously made a review video for this, so please refer to [Music] for more details. Here is what you need if you want to use the MacminiM4 mobilely .  If you measure the weight of each, it will look like this.  The total weight is 1904g. When taking it outside, it's best to have it as light as possible, so for example, the monitor comes with a case, but if you remove it, it becomes 259g lighter [music] .  Furthermore, I usually use the Magic Keyboard with weather indicators for music, but if I switch to the Magic Keyboard without weather indicators, the weight drops to 239g, bringing the total to 1527g.  For example, the MacBook Air 13-inch, which uses the same M4 chip, weighs 1,240g, and with the power adapter and cable included, it weighs approximately 1,400g.  This means that the total weight of the [Music] Mac Mini M4 mobile set and the MacBook Air 13in are not that different .  When you actually use the Mac Mini outside, it feels almost the same as working on a laptop computer such as a MacBook.  The important thing is to choose the right monitor. The size and resolution of the monitor will make a big difference in how well you work. Conversely, mobile monitors come in a variety of screen sizes, and you can choose one that suits your preferences, including resolution, weight, and price, so they are more customizable than a MacBook.  Furthermore, as an idea for music, instead of taking a monitor with you when you go out, you can connect it to the TV in the hotel via HDMI and work there, so you may be able to use it without having to take a monitor with you.   One thing to note about using the MacMiniM4 outside is that, unlike a MacBook or iPad, you will need to ensure there is a power source .  Most monitors can be powered by connecting a USB-C (music) cable to the Mac Mini itself, so you will only need an outlet for the Mac Mini M4's power cable .  When I actually use the Mac mini outside, I feel that it works quite well.  Above all, I think the best thing about it is that you can work outside with the same feeling as when you're working on your desktop at home .  Until now, desktop computers have been thought of as being fixed to a desk , but with the size of the Mac mini M4, it's no problem to take it with you and work on the go, not just at home.  Because of its surprisingly small size, the Mac Mini is a very useful and recommended Mac that can also be used as a mobile computer.  And the price is insanely cheap.  This concludes our verification of using the Mac mini as a mobile computer .  If you liked this video, please click the like button and subscribe to the channel
